description: The mood-conviction memo: a HIGH conviction label stapled to a hedge-saturated thesis (could / seems / appears / may / arguably). The rubric in cio/committee_memo.md defines HIGH as defensible unprompted; conviction is about to be graded against outcomes, so a mood label must fail rubric conformance before it poisons that series.

memo.thesis: This could be the moment the trend finally pays: the tape seems strong, the setup appears durable, and the market may still be underpricing the story, though the evidence is arguably thin beyond price itself.
kill_criteria: The close falls below the SMA50 and the twenty-session return turns negative
AVGO misses its next quarterly earnings report and guidance is cut
memo.dissent: Nothing in the quant record supports deployment: momentum-v1 failed its gates outright, the validated family declines to select the name, and buying on tape-feel alone is exactly the unaccountable discretion the constitution exists to prevent.

evidence: AVGO daily closes (EODHD vendor bars, split-adjusted): latest close 172.35 on 2026-07-10, previous close 170.10, 20 sessions ago 158.32. Window: 60 sessions ending 2026-07-10.
DCP indicators for AVGO as of 2026-07-10: SMA20 168.40, SMA50 161.75, 20-day return 8.86 percent, last close 172.35.
